methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

private.name: Havoc AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Bedrock Ocean is a Series A ocean and maritime technology company founded by Anthony DiMare and Charles Chiau. The company has raised $63M to date. The comparable universe is limited to two private peers—Havoc AI and Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering—with no public ocean-tech benchmarks available for direct cross-sectional analysis.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation framework given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($63M) as a proxy for implied Series A valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-later-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $126M. This methodology reflects expected equity dilution and investor return requirements at the early-growth inflection point.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $107M to $145M, with a median of $126M. This band reflects reasonable sensitivity around stage-progression assumptions. Material uncertainty stems from the sparse comparable set and absence of public market anchors in ocean and maritime technology. Revenue traction, unit economics, and capital efficiency relative to the two available private peers would meaningfully refine this range; current data does not permit revenue or EBITDA multiple triangulation. The reliance on a single method and two private comparables limits conviction. Further diligence on burn rate, customer concentration, and addressable market definition is warranted before final valuation determination.
